questions.question: Who has access to the Enterprise connection settings feature?

options.text: All workspace members regardless of plan
options.explanation: Access to this feature is restricted to Workspace Owners specifically on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Workspace Owners on the Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: Only Workspace Owners with an Enterprise Plan can manage these additional connection settings.

options.text: All users on the Pro Plan
options.explanation: This is an Enterprise-only feature and is not available to Pro Plan users.

options.text: Administrators on the Free Plan
options.explanation: The feature is not available on the Free Plan, nor is it restricted by plan for admins on that plan.

questions.question: What is the default setting for installing connections in a workspace?

options.text: Only from approved list
options.explanation: The default setting is 'No restrictions', meaning all members can install connections.

options.text: No restrictions
options.explanation: By default, the 'No restrictions' option is selected, allowing all workspace members to install any connection.

options.text: Requires domain verification
options.explanation: Domain verification is not required to enable this feature, nor is it part of the default installation settings.

options.text: Requires admin approval for every installation
options.explanation: The default setting does not require admin approval; that is a manual override.

questions.question: If a workspace owner installs an integration that is not on the approved list, what happens automatically?

options.text: The integration is rejected and must be installed again
options.explanation: The integration is not rejected; it is automatically added to the approved list.

options.text: The integration is auto-approved and added to the approved list
options.explanation: When an owner installs a non-approved integration, it is automatically added to the approved list.

options.text: The integration is hidden from all members
options.explanation: The integration is made available to all members once added to the approved list.

options.text: The owner is notified to manually approve the integration
options.explanation: The approval process is automatic for workspace owners installing new integrations.

questions.question: How do you disable the 'Send webhook' action for your entire workspace?

options.text: Go to Automations settings and toggle off webhooks
options.explanation: Webhooks are managed within the Connections tab, not the general Automations settings.

options.text: Go to Settings → Connections and toggle off 'Allow webhooks in automations'
options.explanation: You must navigate to the Connections tab in Settings and toggle the 'Allow webhooks in automations' switch off.

options.text: Contact Notion Support to disable it globally
options.explanation: This is a configuration setting handled by the workspace owner; no support ticket is needed.

options.text: Remove the specific integration causing the webhooks
options.explanation: Disabling webhooks prevents the action from being selected in automations, but does not remove the underlying integration.

questions.question: Which of the following is a prerequisite for adding a connection to the approved list?

options.text: Someone in the workspace must have already installed the connection
options.explanation: To approve a connection for the rest of the workspace, an owner or admin must install it first; otherwise, it won't show up in the dropdown.

options.text: The connection must be built by Notion
options.explanation: This rule applies specifically to auto-approval for Notion-built connections, not the initial approval process.

options.text: Domain verification must be completed
options.explanation: Domain verification is not required to enable this feature or approve connections.

options.text: All workspace members must vote to approve it
options.explanation: Approval is managed solely by the workspace owner, not a vote from members.

questions.question: Under which specific condition can workspace owners see the option to auto-approve connections built by Notion?

options.text: When the 'No restrictions' setting is selected
options.explanation: The auto-approve option is only visible when 'Only from approved list' is selected.

options.text: When the 'Only from approved list' setting is selected
options.explanation: This additional option appears only when the workspace is set to restrict members to an approved list.

options.text: When a third-party integration is installed
options.explanation: Auto-approval for Notion-built connections is a setting related to the approved list mode, not third-party installations.

options.text: When the workspace has more than 50 members
options.explanation: The availability of the auto-approve option is based on the restriction setting, not the number of members.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is NOT available in the menu options (•••) for a specific connection?

options.text: Rename the connection
options.explanation: The menu options include Filter by user, Revoke specific users' access, Disconnect all users, and Remove from approved list, but not Rename.

options.text: Filter by user
options.explanation: This is a valid action available in the menu.

options.text: Revoke specific users' access to a connection
options.explanation: This is a valid action available in the menu.

options.text: Disconnect all users
options.explanation: This is a valid action available in the menu.

questions.question: What does the 'Disconnect all users' option in the connection menu do?

options.text: Removes the connection from the approved list
options.explanation: Removing from the approved list is a separate action. 'Disconnect all users' revokes access for all users currently using the connection.

options.text: Revokes access for all users currently using the connection
options.explanation: This option disconnects all users from the specific connection, preventing them from using it.

options.text: Deletes the connection from the workspace entirely
options.explanation: Disconnecting users does not remove the connection from the workspace or the approved list.

options.text: Requires all users to re-authenticate
options.explanation: It simply revokes access; it does not trigger a re-authentication flow.

questions.question: What information is displayed next to a connection's name in the Approved connections list?

options.text: The date the connection was approved
options.explanation: The list displays the name(s) of the users who have installed the connection, not the approval date.

options.text: The version number of the connection
options.explanation: Version numbers are not displayed in this specific list view.

options.text: The name(s) of the users who have installed the connection
options.explanation: The list shows the specific users who have installed the connection in the workspace.

options.text: The connection's API key
options.explanation: Sensitive information like API keys is never displayed in the UI.

questions.question: What is the immediate effect of turning off 'Allow webhooks in automations'?

options.text: Webhooks stop working for all users across all workspaces
options.explanation: This setting only affects the current workspace; it does not impact webhooks globally.

options.text: Members can no longer create automations at all
options.explanation: Members can still create automations, but they simply cannot select the 'Send webhook' action within those automations.

options.text: Members can't select 'Send webhook' as an action for automations
options.explanation: Disabling this toggle removes the ability to choose 'Send webhook' as an action for any automation within the workspace.

options.text: The existing automations that use webhooks will be deleted
options.explanation: Existing automations are not deleted; they just cannot be triggered via webhooks anymore.
